channel.title: AbuseIPDB on bubka hacks stuff
channel.description: Recent content in AbuseIPDB on bubka hacks stuff

item.title: LD-VPN Connection Detected from Unauthorized Country
item.description: An external IP from Vietnam attempted to authenticate to the corporate VPN as monica@letsdefend.io. MFA blocked access after an incorrect OTP submission. The IP is flagged for SSH brute-force activity. No systems compromised.

item.title: LD-Detected Suspicious Xls File
item.description: A user executed a malicious XLSM file that exploited CVE-2017-11882 via the Equation Editor, dropped and ran an obfuscated VBS payload hidden in an NTFS alternate data stream, and connected to a Brazilian C2 domain flagged by 8/91 VirusTotal vendors.

item.title: LD-Lumma Stealer - DLL Side-Loading via Click Fix Phishing
item.description: A Click Fix phishing email impersonating Microsoft lured Dylan into visiting a malicious site, which executed a disguised PowerShell command launching mshta.exe to download Lumma Stealer payload from overcoatpassably.shop. The host was contained before confirmed data exfiltration.

item.title: Splunk-GoldenSpray
item.description: An attacker from 77.91.78.115 brute-forced mwilliams credentials, connected via RDP, dropped OfficeUpdater.exe with registry persistence, staged Backup_Tools including mimikatz, dumped lsass to obtain jsmith credentials, laterally moved to ST-DC01 and ST-FS01, established scheduled task persistence on the DC, and archived client data for exfiltration.

item.title: LD-Javascript Code Detected in Requested URL
item.description: An external IP performed XSS reconnaissance against the /search/ endpoint, cycling through multiple injection payloads. All requests except the first returned HTTP 302, indicating server-side sanitization blocked execution. The attack did not succeed.

item.title: LD-Passwd Found in Requested URL - Possible LFI Attack
item.description: An external Tencent Cloud IP sent a single LFI request targeting /etc/passwd via path traversal. The server returned HTTP 500 with an empty response body, confirming the attack did not succeed.

item.title: LD-Possible IDOR Attack Detected
item.description: External IP enumerated the /get_user_info/ endpoint via sequential IDOR requests, all returning HTTP 200 - confirming successful data exfiltration across five user accounts.

item.title: LD-Whoami Command Detected in Request Body
item.description: An external attacker from a CHINANET-hosted IP (61.177.172.87) exploited a command injection vulnerability on WebServer1004, executing five OS commands via the ?c= parameter against /video/ - including cat /etc/passwd and cat /etc/shadow - all of which returned HTTP 200 with distinct response sizes, confirming successful remote code execution. The case was escalated to Tier 2.

item.title: LD-Arbitrary File Read on Checkpoint Security Gateway (CVE-2024-24919)
item.description: An attacker exploited CVE-2024-24919 against a Check Point Security Gateway, successfully reading /etc/passwd via a path traversal payload. A second request targeting /etc/shadow from a related IP was blocked. The attack succeeded and the endpoint was escalated to Tier 2.

item.title: CDEF-HawkEye
item.description: A victim host downloaded a HawkEye Keylogger dropper via HTTP, which established persistence, periodically checked the external IP via bot.whatismyipaddress.com, and exfiltrated harvested credentials every 10 minutes over SMTP.

item.title: LD-CVE-2025-53770 SharePoint ToolShell Auth Bypass and RCE
item.description: An attacker exploited CVE-2025-53770 against a SharePoint server, achieving unauthenticated RCE via .NET deserialization. The attacker extracted the MachineKey, compiled and dropped a payload, planted a webshell in the SharePoint layouts directory, and established a reverse connection to the attacker-controlled server.

item.title: LD-Possible SQL Injection Payload Detected
item.description: An external IP hosted on DigitalOcean performed a manual SQL injection reconnaissance against an internal web server, cycling through classic SQLi payloads. All requests returned HTTP 500, confirming the attack did not succeed.

item.title: LD-Deceptive Mail Detected
item.description: A phishing email with a password-protected ZIP delivered AsyncRAT via a SILENTBUILDER dropper. The victim executed the payload, establishing an active C2 channel and triggering full host reconnaissance before containment.
